heading: Educational flexibility program
label: 5891b Educational flexibility program

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Education Flexibility Partnership Act of 1999, and not as part of the Goals 2000: Educate America Act which comprises this chapter.

Amendments2015— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section consisted of subsecs. (a) to (e) relating to the educational flexibility program, programs included in the educational flexibility program, limitations on waivers of program requirements, treatment of existing Ed-Flex Partnership States, and publication of notice of the Secretary’s decision to authorize State educational agencies to issue waivers under this section, respectively.Pub. L. 114–952006—Subsec. (b)(2). substituted “Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006” for “Carl D. Perkins Vocational and Technical Education Act of 1998”.Pub. L. 109–2702002—Subsec. (b). reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The statutory or regulatory requirements referred to in subsection (a)(1)(A) of this section are any such requirements for programs carried out under the following provisions:Pub. L. 107–110“(1) Title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (other than subsections (a) and (c) of section 1116 of such Act).“(2) Part B of title II of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.“(3) Subpart 2 of part A of title III of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (other than section 3136 of such Act).“(4) Title IV of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.“(5) Title VI of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.“(6) Part C of title VII of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.“(7) The Carl D. Perkins Vocational and Technical Education Act of 1998.”

Effective Date of 2015 AmendmentAmendment by effective , except with respect to certain noncompetitive programs and competitive programs, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 114–95section 5 of Pub. L. 114–95section 6301 of this titleDec. 10, 2015

Effective Date of 2002 AmendmentAmendment by effective , except with respect to certain noncompetitive programs and competitive programs, see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 107–110section 5 of Pub. L. 107–110section 6301 of this titleJan. 8, 2002

Educational Flexibility Program Extension, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–211, § 1120 Stat. 320Mar. 24, 2006“(a).—Extension AuthorityNotwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Education is authorized to carry out the educational flexibility program under section 4 of the Education Flexibility Partnership Act of 1999 (), until the date of enactment of an Act that reauthorizes programs under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.), for any State that was an Ed-Flex Partnership State on .20 U.S.C. 5891b20 U.S.C. 6311September 30, 2004“(b)Designation.—“(1).—In generalAny designation of a State as an Ed-Flex Partnership State that was in effect on , shall be extended until the date of enactment of an Act that reauthorizes programs under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965, if the Secretary of Education makes the determination described in paragraph (2).September 30, 2004“(2).—DeterminationThe determination referred to in paragraph (1) is a determination that the performance of the State educational agency, in carrying out the programs for which the State has received a waiver under the educational flexibility program, justifies the extension of the designation.”

Additional State Educational Agencies Authorized To Waive Federal Requirements, , , 1321–229; renumbered title I, , , , provided in part:Pub. L. 104–134, title I, § 101(d) [title III]110 Stat. 1321–211Pub. L. 104–140, § 1(a)110 Stat. 1327Apr. 26, 1996May 2, 1996“That notwithstanding [], the Secretary is authorized to grant up to six additional State education agencies authority to waive Federal statutory or regulatory requirements for fiscal year 1996 and succeeding fiscal years”.section 311(e) of Public Law 103–22720 U.S.C. 5891(e)

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may carry out an educational flexibility program under which the Secretary authorizes a State educational agency that serves an eligible State to waive statutory or regulatory requirements applicable to one or more programs described in subsection (b), other than requirements described in subsection (c), for any local educational agency, educational service agency, or school within the State.

children.heading: Designation
children.label: B Designation
children.text: Each eligible State participating in the program described in subparagraph (A) shall be known as an “Ed-Flex Partnership State”.

children.label: i
children.text: developed and implemented the challenging State academic standards, and aligned assessments, described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of section 1111(b) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [], and is producing the report cards required by section 1111(h) of such Act; or20 U.S.C. 6311(b)

children.label: ii
children.text: if the State has adopted new challenging State academic standards under section 1111(b)(1) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965, as a result of the amendments made to such Act [ et seq.] by the Every Student Succeeds Act, made substantial progress (as determined by the Secretary) toward developing and implementing such standards and toward producing the report cards required under section 1111(h) of such Act;20 U.S.C. 6301

children.label: B
children.text: will hold local educational agencies, educational service agencies, and schools accountable for meeting the educational goals described in the local applications submitted under paragraph (4) and for engaging in technical assistance and, as applicable and appropriate, implementing comprehensive support and improvement activities and targeted support and improvement activities under section 1111(d) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965; and

children.label: C
children.text: waives State statutory or regulatory requirements relating to education while holding local educational agencies, educational service agencies, or schools within the State that are affected by such waivers accountable for the performance of the students who are affected by such waivers.

children.label: I
children.text: Federal statutory or regulatory requirements as described in paragraph (1)(A); and

children.label: II
children.text: State statutory or regulatory requirements relating to education;

children.label: ii
children.text: a detailed description of the State statutory and regulatory requirements relating to education that the State educational agency will waive;

children.label: iii
children.text: a description of clear educational objectives the State intends to meet under the educational flexibility plan, which may include innovative methods to leverage resources to improve program efficiencies that benefit students;

children.label: iv
children.text: a description of how the educational flexibility plan is coordinated with activities described in subsections (b), (c), and (d) of section 1111 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965;

children.label: v
children.text: a description of how the State educational agency will evaluate (consistent with the requirements of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [ et seq.]) the performance of students in the schools, educational service agencies, and local educational agencies affected by the waivers; and20 U.S.C. 6301

children.label: vi
children.text: a description of how the State educational agency will meet the requirements of paragraph (7).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: By not later than 90 days after the date on which a State has submitted an application described in subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall issue a written decision that explains why such application has been approved or disapproved, and the process for revising and resubmitting the application for reconsideration.

children.label: I
children.text: the eligibility of the State as described in paragraph (2);

children.label: II
children.text: the comprehensiveness and quality of the educational flexibility plan described in subparagraph (A);

children.label: III
children.text: the ability of the educational flexibility plan to ensure accountability for the activities and goals described in such plan;

children.label: aa
children.text: are clear and have the ability to be assessed; and

children.label: bb
children.text: take into account the performance of local educational agencies, educational service agencies, or schools, and students, particularly those affected by waivers;

children.label: V
children.text: the significance of the State statutory or regulatory requirements relating to education that will be waived; and

children.label: VI
children.text: the quality of the State educational agency’s process for approving applications for waivers of Federal statutory or regulatory requirements as described in paragraph (1)(A) and for monitoring and evaluating the results of such waivers.

children.label: i
children.text: indicate each Federal program affected and each statutory or regulatory requirement that will be waived;

children.label: ii
children.text: describe the purposes and overall expected results of waiving each such requirement, which may include innovative methods to leverage resources to improve program efficiencies that benefit students;

children.label: iii
children.text: describe, for each school year, specific, measurable, educational goals for each local educational agency, educational service agency, or school affected by the proposed waiver, and for the students served by the local educational agency, educational service agency, or school who are affected by the waiver;

children.label: iv
children.text: explain why the waiver will assist the local educational agency, educational service agency, or school in reaching such goals; and

children.label: v
children.text: in the case of an application from a local educational agency or educational service agency, describe how the agency will meet the requirements of paragraph (7).

children.heading: Evaluation of applications
children.label: B Evaluation of applications
children.text: A State educational agency shall evaluate an application submitted under subparagraph (A) in accordance with the State’s educational flexibility plan described in paragraph (3)(A).

children.label: I
children.text: is applicable to such agency or school, respectively; and

children.label: II
children.text: may include innovative methods to leverage resources to improve program efficiencies that benefit students;

children.label: ii
children.text: the waiver of Federal statutory or regulatory requirements as described in paragraph (1)(A) will assist the local educational agency, educational service agency, or school in reaching its educational goals, particularly goals with respect to school and student performance; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the State educational agency is satisfied that the underlying purposes of the statutory requirements of each program for which a waiver is granted will continue to be met.

children.label: i
children.text: there is compelling evidence of systematic waste, fraud, or abuse;

children.label: ii
children.text: the performance of the local educational agency, educational service agency, or school with respect to meeting the accountability requirement described in paragraph (2)(C) and the goals described in subparagraph (A)(iii) has been inadequate to justify continuation of such waiver;

children.label: iii
children.text: student achievement in the local educational agency, educational service agency, or school has decreased; or

children.label: iv
children.text: substantial progress has not been made toward meeting the long-term goals and measurements of interim progress established by the State under section 1111(c)(4)(A)(i) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.

children.heading: Oversight
children.label: A Oversight
children.text: Each State educational agency participating in the educational flexibility program under this section shall annually monitor the activities of local educational agencies, educational service agencies, and schools receiving waivers under this section.

children.heading: Annual reports
children.label: i Annual reports
children.text: The State educational agency shall submit to the Secretary an annual report on the results of such oversight and the impact of the waivers on school and student performance.

children.label: I
children.text: information on the total number of waivers granted for Federal and State statutory and regulatory requirements under this section, including the number of waivers granted for each type of waiver;

children.label: II
children.text: information describing the effect of the waivers on the implementation of State and local educational reforms pertaining to school and student performance;

children.label: III
children.text: information describing the relationship of the waivers to the performance of schools and students affected by the waivers; and

children.label: IV
children.text: an assurance from State program managers that the data reported under this section are reliable, complete, and accurate, as defined by the State, or a description of a plan for improving the reliability, completeness, and accuracy of such data as defined by the State.

children.label: i
children.text: make each State report submitted under subparagraph (B) available to Congress and the public; and

children.label: ii
children.text: submit to Congress a report that summarizes the State reports and describes the effects that the educational flexibility program under this section had on the implementation of State and local educational reforms and on the performance of students affected by the waivers.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: i Duration
children.text: The Secretary shall approve the application of a State educational agency under paragraph (3) for a period of not more than 5 years.

children.label: I
children.text: completed the performance review of the State educational agency’s educational flexibility plan as described in subparagraph (B); and

children.label: II
children.text: issued a final decision on any pending request for renewal that was submitted by the State educational agency.

children.label: I
children.text: has been effective in enabling such State or affected local educational agencies, educational service agencies, or schools to carry out their State or local reform plans and to continue to meet the accountability requirement described in paragraph (2)(C); and

children.label: II
children.text: has improved student performance.

children.label: I
children.text: has achieved, or is making substantial progress towards achieving, the objectives described in the application submitted pursuant to paragraph (3)(A)(iii) and the specific long-term goals and measurements of interim progress established under section 1111(c)(4)(A)(i) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965; and

children.label: II
children.text: demonstrates that local educational agencies, educational service agencies, or schools affected by the waiver authority or waivers have achieved, or are making progress toward achieving, the desired goals described in the application submitted pursuant to paragraph (4)(A)(iii).

children.heading: Termination of authority
children.label: ii Termination of authority
children.text: The Secretary shall terminate the authority of a State educational agency to grant waivers of Federal statutory or regulatory requirements as described in paragraph (1)(A) if the Secretary determines, after providing the State educational agency with notice and an opportunity for a hearing, that such agency’s performance has been inadequate to justify continuation of such authority based on such agency’s performance against the specific long-term goals and measurements of interim progress established under section 1111(c)(4)(A)(i) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each State educational agency desiring to renew an approved educational flexibility program under this section shall submit a request for renewal to the Secretary not later than the date of expiration of the approved educational flexibility program.

children.heading: Timing for renewal
children.label: ii Timing for renewal
children.text: The Secretary shall either approve or deny the request for renewal by not later than 90 days after completing the performance review of the State described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: I
children.text: has made progress toward achieving the objectives described in the State application submitted pursuant to paragraph (3)(A)(iii); and

children.label: II
children.text: demonstrates in the request that local educational agencies, educational service agencies, or schools affected by the waiver authority or waivers have made progress toward achieving the desired goals described in the local application submitted pursuant to paragraph (4)(A)(iii).

children.label: I
children.text: there is compelling evidence of systematic waste, fraud or abuse; or

children.label: II
children.text: after notice and an opportunity for a hearing, such agency’s performance (including performance with respect to meeting the objectives described in paragraph (3)(A)(iii)) has been inadequate to justify continuation of such authority.

children.heading: Limited compliance period
children.label: ii Limited compliance period
children.text: A State whose authority to grant such waivers has been terminated shall have not more than 1 additional fiscal year to come into compliance in order to seek renewal of the authority to grant waivers under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: shall provide the public with adequate and efficient notice of the proposed waiver authority or waiver, consisting of a description of the agency’s application for the proposed waiver authority or waiver on each agency’s website, including a description of any improved student performance that is expected to result from the waiver authority or waiver;

children.label: B
children.text: shall provide the opportunity for parents, educators, school administrators, and all other interested members of the community to comment regarding the proposed waiver authority or waiver;

children.label: C
children.text: shall provide the opportunity described in subparagraph (B) in accordance with any applicable State law specifying how the comments may be received, and how the comments may be reviewed by any member of the public; and

children.label: D
children.text: shall submit the comments received with the application of the agency or school to the Secretary or the State educational agency, as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: Part A of title I [ et seq.] (other than section 1111).20 U.S.C. 6311

children.label: B
children.text: Part C of title I [ et seq.].20 U.S.C. 6391

children.label: C
children.text: Part D of title I [ et seq.].20 U.S.C. 6421

children.label: D
children.text: Part A of title II [ et seq.].20 U.S.C. 6611

children.label: E
children.text: Part A of title IV [ et seq.].20 U.S.C. 7101
The Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006 ( et seq.).20 U.S.C. 2301

children.label: A
children.text: maintenance of effort;

children.label: B
children.text: comparability of services;

children.label: C
children.text: equitable participation of students and professional staff in private schools;

children.label: D
children.text: parental participation and involvement;

children.label: E
children.text: distribution of funds to States or to local educational agencies;

children.label: F
children.text: serving eligible school attendance areas in rank order in accordance with section 1113(a)(3) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [];20 U.S.C. 6313(a)(3)

children.label: G
children.text: the selection of a school attendance area or school under subsections (a) and (b) of section 1113 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965, except that a State educational agency may grant a waiver to allow a school attendance area or school to participate in activities under part A of title I of such Act [ et seq.] if the percentage of children from low-income families in the school attendance area of such school or who attend such school is not less than 10 percentage points below the lowest percentage of such children for any school attendance area or school of the local educational agency that meets the requirements of such subsections;20 U.S.C. 6311

children.label: H
children.text: use of Federal funds to supplement, not supplant, non-Federal funds; and

children.label: I
children.text: applicable civil rights requirements; and

unless the State educational agency can demonstrate that the underlying purposes of the statutory requirements of the program for which a waiver is granted continue to be met to the satisfaction of the Secretary.

children.heading: Treatment of existing Ed-Flex Partnership States
children.label: d Treatment of existing Ed-Flex Partnership States

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any designation of a State as an Ed-Flex Partnership State that was in effect on , shall be immediately extended for a period of not more than 5 years, if the Secretary makes the determination described in paragraph (2).December 10, 2015

children.heading: Determination
children.label: 2 Determination
children.text: The determination referred to in paragraph (1) is a determination that the performance of the State educational agency, in carrying out the programs for which the State has received a waiver under the educational flexibility program, justifies the extension of the designation.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: e Publication
children.text: A notice of the Secretary’s decision to authorize State educational agencies to issue waivers under this section, including a description of the rationale the Secretary used to approve applications under subsection (a)(3)(B), shall be published in the Federal Register and the Secretary shall provide for the dissemination of such notice to State educational agencies, interested parties (including educators, parents, students, and advocacy and civil rights organizations), and the public.
